# @zakkster/lite-headless/sortable

Headless drag-to-reorder list with keyboard fallback.

## Quick example

```js
import { createSortable } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/sortable";

const sortable = createSortable({
    items: ["task-1", "task-2", "task-3"],
    onReorder: (newOrder, info) => {
        // commit your state. info: { reason, from, to, key }
        console.log(newOrder, info);
    },
});

sortable.attachRoot(listEl, { label: "Reorder tasks" });
itemEls.forEach((el, i) => sortable.attachItem(el, taskIds[i]));

// optional: register a grip handle so drag only starts when the
// user grabs the dots, not the whole row
sortable.attachHandle(handleEl, "task-1");
```

## Two modes

**Framework mode (default)**: the primitive emits `onReorder` events
but never moves DOM nodes. Your framework (React, Vue, Svelte, etc.)
already owns the rendering of the list ŌĆö when the event fires, you
update your state and the framework re-renders. No DOM thrash, no
double-update.

**Vanilla mode** (`applyDOMReorder: true`): the primitive calls
`appendChild` to physically reorder item elements in their parent on
every commit. Use this in plain HTML pages without a framework. The
DOM order will always match `sortable.items()` after a commit.

## Options

| Option              | Default     | Notes                                       |
| ------------------- | ----------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| `orientation`       | `"vertical"`| `"vertical"` or `"horizontal"`              |
| `items`             | `[]`        | initial order (array of string keys)        |
| `applyDOMReorder`   | `false`     | move DOM nodes on commit (vanilla mode)     |
| `keyboardEnabled`   | `true`      | Space/arrow pickup-and-move flow            |
| `disabled`          | `false`     | block all drag + keyboard                   |
| `dragStartThreshold`| `5`         | px the pointer must move before drag starts |
| `announceLive`      | `true`      | build a visually hidden aria-live region    |
| `onReorder`         | -           | `(newOrder, info) => void`                  |
| `onDragStart`       | -           | `(key) => void`                             |
| `onDragEnd`         | -           | `(key, committed) => void`                  |

`info.reason` values: `"api"`, `"api-swap"`, `"drag"`, `"keyboard"`,
`"insert"`, `"remove"`, `"set"`.

## Attachment methods

| Method                       | Returns      | Notes                              |
| ---------------------------- | ------------ | ---------------------------------- |
| `attachRoot(el, { label? })` | cleanup `()` | role="listbox", aria-orientation, hidden aria-live region |
| `attachItem(el, key, opts?)` | cleanup `()` | `opts: { disabled }`. tabindex=0 added if absent |
| `attachHandle(el, key)`      | cleanup `()` | restricts drag start to this element |

## Imperative API

```js
sortable.move(key, toIndex)        // move existing key to index
sortable.swap(keyA, keyB)          // swap two keys
sortable.setOrder([...])           // replace entire order
sortable.insertAt(key, atIndex)    // add a new key
sortable.removeKey(key)            // remove a key
sortable.setDisabled(flag)         // disable / enable whole list
sortable.setItemDisabled(key, flag)
sortable.items()                   // current order array
sortable.isDragging()              // pointer or keyboard drag in flight
sortable.dragKey()                 // key currently being dragged
sortable.destroy()
```

Reactive accessors (return signal getters; subscribe inside an `effect`):

```js
sortable.order()       // signal -> current array of keys
sortable.dragging()    // signal -> currently-pointer-dragged key, or null
sortable.grabbed()     // signal -> currently-keyboard-grabbed key, or null
```

## Pointer drag flow

1. **`pointerdown`** on an item (or its registered handle): record
   the start position. No visible state change yet.
2. **`pointermove`** past `dragStartThreshold` pixels (5 by default):
   drag begins. The item gets `data-dragging="true"`. The primitive
   reads every other item's `getBoundingClientRect()` *once* and
   caches the midpoints. Subsequent moves do not re-read the DOM.
3. **`pointermove`** during drag: compute target slot via
   cached-midpoint test (pointer-axis coord vs item midpoints).
   The slot's item element gets `data-insert-before="true"` (or
   `data-insert-after`), giving the consumer a CSS hook to draw an
   insertion indicator.
4. **`pointerup`**: commit the reorder if the slot changed.
   `onReorder` fires with `info.reason: "drag"`.
5. **`pointercancel`** or **`Escape`** during drag: revert the
   data-attributes; no reorder commits.

The cache is invalidated on every dragend. Window resize / scroll
during a drag does NOT invalidate (we accept slightly-stale rects
mid-drag for performance; in practice nothing repaints during the
drag because the consumer typically blocks layout shifts).

## Keyboard flow (WAI-ARIA pickup pattern)

1. **Tab** focuses an item.
2. **Space** (or **Enter**) picks up the item. `aria-grabbed="true"`
   is set; the screen reader announces "Picked up item 3. Use arrows
   to move, Space to drop, Escape to cancel."
3. **Arrow Ōåæ/Ōåō** (vertical) or **ŌåÉ/ŌåÆ** (horizontal) moves the picked-
   up item by one slot. Each move commits ŌĆö `onReorder` fires with
   `info.reason: "keyboard"`.
4. **Home / End** jump to first / last position.
5. **Space** (or **Enter**) drops. `aria-grabbed` is removed.
6. **Escape** ends pickup mode. **Note**: tentative moves committed
   during pickup are NOT reverted (the primitive doesn't keep an
   undo stack; each arrow move IS a commit). If you need rollback,
   snapshot the order at `onDragStart` and restore on
   `onDragEnd(_, committed=false)`.

## ARIA painted attributes

| Element       | Attributes                                       |
| ------------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
| Root          | `role="listbox"`, `aria-orientation`, `aria-label`, `data-orientation` |
| Item          | `role="option"`, `aria-grabbed` (during pickup), `aria-disabled`, `data-dragging`, `data-insert-before` / `data-insert-after` |
| Handle        | `data-sortable-handle="true"`, `cursor: grab`     |

A visually hidden `<div aria-live="polite">` is appended to the root
for announcements during keyboard pickup ("Picked up item 3",
"Item now at position 5", "Dropped"). Disable with
`announceLive: false` if you have your own announcer.

## Element wrapper

```html
<lite-sortable label="Reorder tasks">
    <ul>
        <li data-sortable-item="task-1">
            <span data-sortable-handle>Ōŗ«Ōŗ«</span>
            Buy milk
        </li>
        <li data-sortable-item="task-2">Walk dog</li>
        <li data-sortable-item="task-3" data-disabled>Pay rent</li>
    </ul>
</lite-sortable>

<script>
    document.querySelector("lite-sortable").addEventListener("reorder", (e) => {
        // e.detail.order   -- new array of keys
        // e.detail.info    -- { reason, from, to, key, ... }
    });
</script>
```

Reactive attributes:

- `disabled` ŌĆö toggles the whole list

Read-once attributes:

- `label`, `orientation`, `apply-dom-reorder`, `no-keyboard`

Dispatched events:

- `reorder` ŌĆö `{ detail: { order, info } }`
- `dragstart` ŌĆö `{ detail: { key } }`
- `dragend` ŌĆö `{ detail: { key, committed } }`

## Pairing with `@zakkster/lite-store` and `@zakkster/lite-crdt`

For non-trivial state ŌĆö multi-tab sync, multi-user collaboration,
optimistic updates with a server ŌĆö pair sortable with the store /
CRDT layer:

```js
import { createStore } from "@zakkster/lite-store";
import { ORSet } from "@zakkster/lite-crdt";

const store = createStore({ taskOrder: ["t1", "t2", "t3"] });

// sync sortable -> store (drives downstream consumers via per-key
// signals; framework re-renders the list)
const sortable = createSortable({
    items: store.taskOrder.slice(),
    onReorder: (newOrder) => { store.taskOrder = newOrder; },
});
```

For multi-user reorder over BroadcastChannel / WebSocket, the same
pattern wraps `ORSet` with a per-op sequencer for stable
convergence. The sortable primitive itself remains transport-
agnostic ŌĆö it just emits events.

## Recommended CSS

```css
[data-sortable-item] {
    user-select: none;          /* don't let text-select interfere with drag */
    -webkit-user-select: none;
    touch-action: none;         /* don't let touch-pan compete with drag */
}
```

The primitive also calls `e.preventDefault()` on pointerdown
(unless the press lands on an input/textarea/contenteditable
descendant) and on every pointermove during a pending drag, so
text selection won't start during the pre-threshold ramp. The CSS
above is the belt; the primitive's preventDefault is the
suspenders.

## Animating reorders (FLIP recipe)

Reorder events are point-in-time ŌĆö the dragged item teleports from
its old slot to its new one. For a smoother UX, layer a FLIP
animation on top: capture rects BEFORE the commit, do the commit,
read NEW rects AFTER, translate items back to where they were, then
animate the transform to zero.

With **GSAP Flip** (lightweight, opinionated):

```js
import { gsap } from "gsap";
import { Flip } from "gsap/Flip";
gsap.registerPlugin(Flip);

const container = document.getElementById("list");
const sortable  = document.getElementById("sortable");

sortable.addEventListener("dragstart", () => {
    // Capture state BEFORE the commit. We snapshot here rather than
    // in onReorder because the dragstart event fires while items
    // are still in their pre-drag positions.
    window._flipState = Flip.getState(container.children);
});
sortable.addEventListener("reorder", () => {
    // applyDOMReorder has already moved the nodes; animate from
    // the captured pre-drag state to the new one.
    Flip.from(window._flipState, {
        duration: 0.4,
        ease: "power1.inOut",
        stagger: 0.03,
    });
});
```

With **zero deps** (rolling your own FLIP in ~30 lines):

```js
function flipBefore(els) {
    const rects = new Map();
    for (const el of els) rects.set(el, el.getBoundingClientRect());
    return rects;
}
function flipAfter(rects) {
    for (const [el, before] of rects) {
        const after = el.getBoundingClientRect();
        const dx = before.left - after.left;
        const dy = before.top  - after.top;
        if (dx === 0 && dy === 0) continue;
        el.style.transition = "none";
        el.style.transform  = `translate(${dx}px, ${dy}px)`;
        // next frame: clear transition, let it animate to identity
        requestAnimationFrame(() => requestAnimationFrame(() => {
            el.style.transition = "transform 0.3s ease";
            el.style.transform  = "";
        }));
    }
}

let _flip = null;
sortable.addEventListener("dragstart", () => {
    _flip = flipBefore(container.children);
});
sortable.addEventListener("reorder", () => {
    if (_flip) { flipAfter(_flip); _flip = null; }
});
```

Same FLIP technique works for any reorder source ŌĆö `move`, `swap`,
`setOrder`, shuffle buttons. Wrap your mutation in the
before/after pattern. The dragstart/dragend events only fire for
pointer + keyboard pickup, so for imperative API calls, capture
manually:

```js
function withFlip(mutate) {
    const state = flipBefore(container.children);
    mutate();
    flipAfter(state);
}

withFlip(() => sortable.setOrder(shuffled));
```

Kept out of the primitive intentionally ŌĆö animation timing,
easing, and stagger are an aesthetic choice the consumer should
own.


## CSS contract

This primitive's painted attributes follow the canonical CSS contract
in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. See that doc for the cross-primitive
taxonomy (booleans like `data-open` / `data-disabled` / `data-loading`,
enums like `data-status` / `data-side`, ARIA pairings, host accessor
naming, and event naming).
